intro: Homoioceltis aspera, also known as the Asian bitter melon, is a plant species in the Cucurbitaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing blood sugar levels, improving lipid profiles, and exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Homoioceltis aspera include cucurbitacins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Homoioceltis aspera include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes and hypertension.
